runs.author: ai
runs.hypothesis: Deeper imposed curvature well pulls the shell closer (smaller well_distance_min) but does not, by itself, change induced spin.
falsifiers: well_distance_min does not decrease as well_depth increases
max_abs_spin changes strongly with well_depth at fixed asymmetry
runs.interpretation: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED / new coupling found. Deeper wells did NOT monotonically reduce well_distance_min (3.098 -> 2.960 -> 3.043), and spin rose strongly with depth (0.080 -> 0.134 -> 0.205). Cause: the auto orbit-velocity is derived from well_depth, so depth and shell torque are coupled through the initial condition. Next test must hold orbit velocity fixed while varying depth to separate the two effects.

runs.author: ai
runs.hypothesis: When orbit-launch velocity is held FIXED across cases, increasing imposed well_depth pulls the shell closer (smaller well_distance_min) and does NOT, by itself, drive induced spin. This isolates well depth from the orbit-velocity confound found in EXP-2026-07-23-002, where the auto orbit velocity was derived from well_depth (0.475 -> 0.619 -> 0.765) and therefore co-varied with torque.
falsifiers: well_distance_min does not decrease monotonically as well_depth increases at fixed orbit velocity
max_abs_spin still varies strongly (> 0.05 spread) with well_depth once orbit velocity is held fixed
runs.interpretation: HYPOTHESIS LARGELY CONFIRMED, and it resolves the EXP-2026-07-23-002 confound. With orbit-launch velocity pinned at vy=0.6188 and identical start position for all three cases, well_distance_min is now MONOTONIC in depth: 4.392 -> 2.961 -> 2.419 for well_depth 1.0 -> 1.7 -> 2.6. Deeper imposed wells pull the shell closer, as expected. This removes the non-monotonicity seen in EXP-002 (3.098 -> 2.960 -> 3.043), confirming that anomaly was an artifact of the auto orbit velocity co-varying with depth (0.475 -> 0.619 -> 0.765), not a real depth effect. Induced spin is now NON-monotonic and only weakly coupled to depth: max_abs_spin 0.085 -> 0.134 -> 0.103 (spread 0.049, versus 0.125 in EXP-002 where launch speed rose with depth). So the strong depth->spin coupling reported in EXP-002 was mostly the faster launch, not the well itself; at fixed launch velocity, depth does not monotonically drive spin. The small residual variation is consistent with deeper wells changing the orbit geometry (dist_max stays ~4.62 while dist_min drops), which modestly changes how the pressure/compression front shears the lattice. Falsifier 1 (dist_min not monotonic) is NOT triggered. Falsifier 2 (spin spread > 0.05) is borderline (0.049) and not triggered; report as weak-but-present coupling rather than none. Net: well DEPTH controls capture distance; orbit LAUNCH velocity was the dominant driver of induced spin. Next: sweep launch velocity at fixed depth to confirm the velocity->spin channel directly.

runs.author: ai
runs.hypothesis: Increasing shell asymmetry increases induced axial spin at fixed orbit.
falsifiers: max_abs_spin does not increase monotonically with asymmetry
runs.interpretation: CONFIRMED. Induced axial spin rose monotonically with shell asymmetry (0.080 -> 0.134 -> 0.219) at fixed orbit. The asymmetric shell converts curvature-gradient forces into net torque; the symmetric case would produce none. Falsifier (non-monotonic spin) not triggered. Remains YELLOW: torque comes from an imposed well.
